summary: The user, Analyst 4, conducted the final review and formal closure of incident INC-2026-04417. They began by confirming the full completion of the emergency certificate re-rotation (CHG-89104) and the success of subsequent mTLS verification tests, which verified full service recovery. A key focus was definitively disproving the initial theory of database pool exhaustion by reverting a temporary pool size increase and observing sustained health, thus confirming it was a downstream symptom. The analyst investigated and resolved the final open red flags, identifying the root cause as a race condition in PR #4781, which was exacerbated by SAN validation being left in a 'soft-fail' mode due to a previous, unrelated change. After confirming the resolution of all business impacts, including zero financial discrepancy, and reviewing the approved corrective actions, the user formally closed the incident, satisfied that the audit trail was complete and robust.
key_facts: The emergency certificate re-rotation (CHG-89104) was completed for all 119 affected pods.
A mesh-wide mTLS verification test passed for all 312 endpoints post-remediation.
The initial theory of database pool exhaustion was definitively disproven by reverting the pool size to its original 200, which resulted in only 39% utilization and no performance degradation.
The root cause was identified as a race condition in PR #4781 that caused incorrect SANs ('*.mesh.internal' instead of '*.mesh.local') to be issued for some certificates.
A contributing factor was that SAN validation was in 'soft-fail' mode due to a prior change (CHG-84221), which prevented the error from blocking deployment.
All 87,341 indeterminate transactions were reconciled with zero financial discrepancy.
The incident resulted in 23 SLA breaches and an estimated $412,000 in credits.
All 11 red flags raised during the incident were confirmed as resolved before closure.
The incident was formally closed after a total duration of 41 hours and 34 minutes.
The diagnostic theory evolved across shifts, from DB exhaustion (Shift A) to a TLS anomaly (Shift B), and finally to the specific race condition (Shifts C/D).
positions_taken: The user considered the test that disproved the database theory as definitive proof ("Q.E.D.").
The user identified the failure to revert the 'soft-fail' SAN validation setting as a "classic governance gap."
The user stated the incident's diagnostic evolution is a "textbook case" and a "valuable case study" for training.
The user emphasized the importance of documenting the full causal chain, from the code bug to the initial symptom, for the post-mortem.
The user expressed confidence that the audit trail was "complete and robust" before closing the incident.
The user recognized that the temporary relief followed by regression from early mitigations was a strong signal that the root cause was not being addressed.
